ac:rich-text-body.p: SKA is deployed in stages called Array Assemblies, enabling early science and commissioning before full completion.

p: The SKA is deployed in stages called Array Assemblies (AAs). Each AA represents a construction milestone that provides end-to-end telescope functionality, including receiver systems, signal processing, and correlators. This approach enables early science operations, commissioning activities, and procedural development before the array reaches full completion, reducing technical risk and accelerating scientific capability.
The first test array designed for interferometry verification and system-level validation.

ul.li: Prototype dishes and receiver systems
Engineering-focused deployment to discover system-level issues early
Used for commissioning procedures and technical integration

p: First operational array stage with 8 dishes for SKA-Mid.

ul.li: Correlator upgraded to 16k channels
800 MHz imaging bandwidth
Establishes operational procedures for future stages

p: Major expansion phase with significant performance gains and science validation.

ul.li: 56 dishes added to SKA-Mid array (approximately one dish per week integration rate)
Performance comparable to existing radio facilities
Dedicated science verification and validation phase
SKA-Low Science Verification data expected 2027
SKA-Mid Science Verification data expected 2029

p: Represents a planned transition and consolidation point before full array capability.

ul.li: SKA-Mid: 144 dishes total (80 SKA + 64 MeerKAT partnership dishes)
SKA-Low: 257 stations
November 2025 SKAO Council approved deferral of some planned capabilities to balance scope and funding

p: Final expansion phases leading to full array capability.

ul.li: AA4 represents the complete SKA array with all planned elements
Scheduling and funding subject to future decisions and resource availability

p: Science commissioning is proceeding with strong progress on key metrics:

ul.li: Per-antenna sensitivity performing as anticipated
Interferometric fringes successfully demonstrated
Continuum imaging operational
Faraday synthesis spectra capabilities validated
Pulsar timing measurements producing results

p: Construction proceeding on schedule. Currently approximately 2 years behind SKA-Low due to the complexity of the mid-frequency array systems and prototyping phase.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Key Milestone: SKA-Low has achieved full interferometric functionality with all science verification modes operational. This validates the staged delivery approach and provides confidence for SKA-Mid development.

Each Array Assembly stage is designed to support subarray operations, enabling the array to be partitioned into independent observing units for concurrent science observations. Subarray capability allows:

ul.li: Multiple simultaneous science targets at different frequencies
Flexible scheduling of heterogeneous observation types
Improved utilisation of array resources
Support for both survey and deep-field observations in parallel

ac:rich-text-body.p: This operational flexibility is integral to maximizing scientific return across the array's staged deployment.
